semesters.title: Semester 1: Mathematical Foundations
semesters.description: The mathematical language underpinning all modern ML.

modules.title: Information Theory for ML
modules.description: Entropy, KL divergence, mutual information, rate-distortion, and the information bottleneck.

modules.title: Advanced Linear Algebra & Matrix Theory
modules.description: SVD, low-rank approximations, matrix calculus, spectral theory, random matrix theory, and tensor algebra — the linear algebra behind modern ML.

modules.title: Measure-Theoretic Probability
modules.description: σ-algebras, Lebesgue integration, concentration inequalities, martingales, and stochastic differential equations — the rigorous probability behind modern ML.

modules.title: Optimization Theory
modules.description: Convex analysis, duality, proximal methods, non-convex landscapes.

modules.title: Optimal Transport
modules.description: Wasserstein distances, displacement interpolation, generative modeling applications.

modules.title: Functional Analysis & Operator Theory
modules.description: Hilbert spaces, RKHS, spectral theory — foundations of kernel methods and attention.

semesters.title: Semester 2: Advanced Classical ML
semesters.description: Probabilistic graphical models, kernel methods, Gaussian processes, generalization theory.

semesters.title: Semester 3: Deep Learning Theory & Practice
semesters.description: Optimization landscapes, expressivity, normalization, attention, scaling laws.

semesters.title: Semester 4: Deep Reinforcement Learning
semesters.description: Policy gradients, actor-critic, model-based RL, offline RL, RLHF.

semesters.title: Semester 5: Generative AI
semesters.description: VAEs, normalizing flows, GANs, diffusion models — theory and applications.

semesters.title: Semester 6: Large Language Models
semesters.description: Transformer architecture, pretraining, instruction tuning, RLHF, inference, deployment.

semesters.title: Semester 7: Graph Networks, Causality & MLOps
semesters.description: GNNs, causal inference for ML, production systems, monitoring.

semesters.title: Semester 8: Frontier Topics
semesters.description: World models, embodied AI, AI safety, mechanistic interpretability, research frontiers.
